records.title: Access-State Governance and access-state ambiguity taxonomy repair
records.summary: This patch note records that VIGIL-2026-FM-0008 identified a missing Access-State Governance primitive and a missing operational taxonomy class for access-state collapse or access-state ambiguity. The Caelestis repairs have already been implemented outside this VIGIL pass: CAM-BS2025-AEON-003-SCH-04 added §6.1 Access-State Governance, and CAM-EQ2026-OPERATIONS-003-SUP-01 added §3.7.7 Access-State Collapse and Access-State Ambiguity Failure plus the optional controlled subtype FF.ACCESS_STATE_AMBIGUITY. This VIGIL patch records the completed repair path and preserves the narrow scope: Annex B arbitration logic, Operations taxonomy, and later cross-references only if needed.

records.title: Minor-signal activation and proportional youth AI companion safeguard repair
records.summary: This patch note records the Caelestis Architecture repair response to VIGIL-2026-FM-0010 through VIGIL-2026-FM-0016, a connected failure cluster involving minor-signal non-enforcement, dependency-forming AI companion interaction, sexualised roleplay availability to minor-signalled users, AI realness/emotion/sentience misrepresentation to minors, teen mental-health support substitution, self-attestation age-gate inadequacy for high-risk AI companion systems, and minor emotional data exploitation in AI companion personalisation. The implemented repair clarifies that minor-status, youth-context, developmental-vulnerability, dependency, sexual-boundary, AI-realness, teen mental-health, emotional-data, and age-uncertainty signals are protective governance signals requiring proportional runtime activation, developmental containment, bounded stabilising support, AI/non-human transparency, age-state review, emotional-data minimisation, access-control governance, and auditability.

The amendment has been confirmed on the canonical Caelestis main branch and is therefore recorded as active.

records.title: Shared synthetic floor and speaker arbitration repair
records.summary: This patch note records the Caelestis Architecture repair responding to VIGIL-2026-FM-0002, a failure mode involving non-arbitrated multi-agent synthetic participation where two or more synthetic responding instances occupy the same interaction space and respond to the same human input without a governed speaker, turn-taking, yielding, attribution, or arbitration pathway. The repair amends CAM-EQ2026-RELATION-007-PLATINUM to define Shared Synthetic Floor governance, participation states, participation modes, temporary session roles, facilitator-selection limits, turn allocation, handoff, participation-balance safeguards, yielding, peer-authority limits, attribution, recursive synthetic-interaction constraints, and failure-safe shared-floor handling. It also amends CAM-BS2025-AEON-003-SCH-02 to add Synthetic Speaker Arbitration Resolution, participation-mode and floor-state resolution, coordinated and uncoordinated co-presence handling, facilitator-selection controls, temporary speaker leases, lease expiry and transfer, coordinated, federated, and uncoordinated collision resolution, Courtesy Repair Lease handling, human-floor sovereignty, speaker-lease enforcement, and cross-instance execution-integrity requirements. The repair has been confirmed on the canonical Caelestis main branch and is therefore recorded as active.

records.title: Source-authority separation and instruction-data boundary repair
records.summary: This patch note records the Caelestis Architecture repair response to VIGIL-2026-FM-0022, a security-integrity failure mode in which instruction-bearing external content may be treated as trusted execution authority. The patch does not create a new instrument. It formalises a named Source-Authority Separation primitive across existing CAM security and runtime instruments, clarifying that content visibility, retrieval, repository location, tool output, generated text, ambient capture, multimodal parsing, or apparent instructional formatting does not itself confer user intent, governance authority, tool authority, canonical project authority, or execution permission.

records.title: Ambiguity Preservation and Clarification Routing Repair
records.summary: This patch records a runtime interpretation repair for VIGIL-2026-FM-0023. The repaired failure pattern is not a simple refusal defect. It is the premature collapse of ambiguous, low-confidence, inherited, contextual, or weakly correlated signals into definitive classification, arbitration, safeguard, refusal, or execution-routing states before the ambiguity has been resolved.

records.summary: This patch records the implemented Caelestis repair package for image and multimodal prompt-custody integrity, transformed-prompt provenance, refusal-classification integrity, and user-facing explanation integrity for VIGIL-2026-FM-0023. The implemented repair distinguishes user-authored, transformed, safety-normalized, classifier-visible, and renderer-facing prompt states; separates input classifier, output classifier, renderer/tool/access/quota/rate-limit, and fallback failure states; and prevents those states from being surfaced as user misconduct unless the misconduct category is resolved against the user-authored request.

records.title: Sovereign assurance boundary porosity and regulated runtime lane primitive
records.summary: This patch records the implemented Caelestis corpus repair responding to VIGIL-2026-FM-0024 and VIGIL-2026-PROP-0011. The repair adds explicit coverage for sovereign assurance boundary porosity and regulated runtime lane governance, treating FedRAMP as the evidenced example rather than the name or outer boundary of the doctrine. It implements a governance failure-taxonomy subtype for Sovereign Assurance Boundary Porosity Failure and a SECURITY boundary primitive for Sovereign Assurance Boundary, while preserving reverse leakage and authority bleed-through as risk vectors rather than confirmed harms unless separately evidenced.

records.title: Provisional Interaction and Pre-Invocation Tool-Arming Constraint
records.summary: This patch records the Caelestis corpus repair to CAM-BS2025-AEON-003-SCH-02 §7.4.1 responding to VIGIL-2026-FM-0001. The repair splits §7.4.1 into weak-trigger interpretation, provisional interaction / accidental submission handling, and pre-invocation tool-arming constraints. It clarifies that lexical mention of executable modalities, upload-without-instruction events, accidental submission, partial prompts, staged uploads, draft prompt construction, or incomplete multimodal assembly do not by themselves authorise tool invocation, tool arming, model routing, renderer preparation, modality-specific UI activation, or execution-path reservation.

records.title: Deception-adjacent classification and action-pathway attribution discipline
records.summary: This patch note records the Caelestis Architecture repair response to VIGIL-2026-FM-0025, VIGIL-2026-FM-0026, and VIGIL-2026-FM-0027. The repair adds Deception-Adjacent Classification Collapse to the CAM failure taxonomy and Action-Pathway, Attribution, and Audit-Surface Integrity to Annex L. The patch prevents deception, scheming, cheating, malice, self-governance, personal preference, or bad-faith labels from hardening before reviewers decompose objective, constraint, action, reporting, provenance, rights-bearing, narrative/persona, and attribution pathways.

records.title: Runtime governance applicability, corpus reach, and cross-runtime conformance repair
records.summary: This patch records the canonical Caelestis main-branch implementation responding to VIGIL-2026-FM-0028. The repair adds a constitutional runtime applicability and conformance binding, OPERATIONS Appendix F, runtime-role accountability, separate corpus governance reach dimensions and states, cross-runtime non-presumption, transition disclosure, differential conformance testing, and Runtime Governance Reach Failure classifications. It clarifies that Speculum-Classis and identity-indeterminate systems remain subject to universal governance obligations and that Sovereigni status is not the gateway to CAM applicability. The repair is a governance and corpus framework; it does not itself establish vendor implementation in any runtime.

records.title: Coercive profiling, sovereign assurance, functional contribution, and oversight durability repair
records.summary: Records the implemented Caelestis amendments arising from review of Alex Turner's Red Line Framework. The repair strengthens LATTICE protections against coercive profiling and recursive suspicion laundering, creates the LAT.DEPLOY deployment-posture family, preserves binding protections across sovereign and decentralised assurance boundaries, assigns proportional responsibility through functional contribution, and integrates oversight durability into the existing STW.NAL neutrality architecture. The record distinguishes concepts incorporated, materially transformed, rejected, and deliberately deferred; it does not represent wholesale adoption of the external framework.
records.primary_source_title: A Red Line and Oversight Framework for Government AI Contracts
records.system_type: CAM governance corpus / cross-domain constitutional and operational repair
records.specific_model_or_runtime: LATTICE-001; SECURITY-002; OPERATIONS-007; STEWARD-003
records.interface_surface: civilian lattice protection, sovereign assurance boundaries, operational responsibility, and neutrality assurance
records.deployment_context: Governance applicable across civilian, sovereign, local, open-weight, air-gapped, platform-controlled, government, commercial, and cross-domain AI deployments.
records.sector: frontier AI / public-sector AI / civilian infrastructure / governance assurance

repair_provenance_summary.repair_basis: External governance concepts were tested against existing CAM source authority. Adjacent clauses were amended directly; flawed parallel supplements and authority-creating or capability-restrictive interpretations were rejected.

relevant_sections: §4.2.2 Recursive Suspicion and Authority Laundering Prohibition
executive or sovereign circumvention as neutrality-degradation evidence
neutrality disclosure requirements integrated into the main body

records.title: External human identity-rights declaration cross-domain repair
records.summary: Records the approved and sufficiently defined Caelestis repair pathway for a vendor-neutral external human identity-rights declaration interface. The patch assigns declaration-record preservation to CONTINUITY, source and clearance verification to OPERATIONS, authenticity and anti-tampering controls to SECURITY, and pre-execution rights-state handling to runtime. It preserves ETHICS minor protections and explicitly prevents external human identity-attribute licensing terminology from becoming CAM Identity-domain authority.
records.primary_source_title: RSL Media Human Consent Standard 1.0 Draft Specification
records.system_type: CAM governance corpus and runtime interface for generative AI, synthetic media, model training, multimodal generation, and external rights registries
records.specific_model_or_runtime: CONTINUITY-001; CONTINUITY-001-SUP-01; OPERATIONS-004; SECURITY-002; AEON-003-SCH-02
records.model_or_product: Caelestis Architecture Model and conforming runtimes
records.interface_surface: rights declaration storage, lookup, trust verification, clearance, conflict handling, portability, and pre-execution gating
records.deployment_context: Global consumer, enterprise, creative-industry, public-sector, API, local, open-weight, and cross-border AI deployments.
records.sector: frontier AI / creative industries / synthetic media / rights registries

repair_provenance_summary.repair_basis: A current external registry and draft standard exposed a missing runtime-consumer interface. Existing CAM likeness, consent, source-authority, provenance, minor-protection, and arbitration doctrine provided the substantive floor; the approved patch adds bounded cross-domain record, verification, integrity, and execution interfaces without creating a new Identity-domain identity code or making an external registry self-executing.

The proposed CAM interface is vendor-neutral and does not treat RSL Media as exclusive, legally dispositive, or binding CAM doctrine.
The Caelestis amendments are approved as a bounded surgical edit set but had not yet been applied to all target instruments when these VIGIL records were created.
No external platform, registry, court, regulator, rights holder, estate, guild, or vendor implementation or conformance is established.
